reviews.review_text:
Lovely peaceful stay here for 3 nights - I booked a waterfront site. These are small but the booking process makes it clear that you can’t fit much more than a car and a side awning. No other additions. You are obviously close to other campers. Fellow campers here are friendly and respectful.

It was $60/night for unpowered, which I thought was quite pricey, but….

The views of sunrises and sun sets here are beautiful. I went back to camp early so I could enjoy the sunset from camp! There aren’t many people crowding the water front (like at waterfront campsites in other campgrounds) so it feels relatively private.

Bathrooms were pretty clean. However it seems that free campers on the street outside will sneak into the bathrooms. Management has a PIN code to try and manage this. I was showering when I heard a man enter the women’s bathroom with a lady that he’d obviously snuck in with. This made me uncomfortable.

The wifi really does not work.

Camp kitchen has stunning views. There were 3 bbq hot plates and 3 big sinks for wash up. The microwave needs a really good clean.

This spot was really well located to catch the bus to the surrounding areas. It’s only 50c and beats trying to navigate parking at Hastings St. You can also go for a short stroll and catch the ferry nearby.

If you are wanting a quiet, peaceful stay - this might be the place for you.

Gorgeous stay! We stayed on a beachfront unpowered site and were welcomed with a lovely smile and even a young gentleman to tell me all about his cheese twists and show me his drawings! It was nothing short of breathtaking views and the bathrooms were super secure and super clean! I would return in a heartbeat!
Einfacher Campingplatz, zentral gelegen, jedoch sind die Sanierten Anlagen nicht sehr sauber.

Sanitäre Anlagen scheinen nicht täglich geputzt zu werden.
Schöne Lage am Fluss.
All good!
Lovely Caravan Park. Close to all amenities and local tourist spots. Our special spot.

What an amazing Caravan Park this park is it’s everything and more, one of the best parks we have ever stayed at everything is well maintained and very very clean, the happy hours down the front of the waters edge meeting new people everyday and making life long friends. Do yourself a favour and book in here we have re booked for next year.

Unfortunately, our experience at this caravan park was extremely disappointing. We have travelled vastly overseas and in Australia, from Darwing to Sydney ( over 70 caraven/camping sites)

From the beginning, the facilities did not meet basic expectations. There was human waste left in the toilets, several kitchen burners were not functioning properly, and the overall maintenance of common areas appeared neglected.

We stayed as a family of two adults and one child, and quickly realised this campground seems to cater almost exclusively to a much older demographic, with very little understanding or accommodation for families with children. The atmosphere felt more like a silent retirement village than a welcoming holiday park where families can relax, connect, and enjoy nature together.

What saddened us the most was the treatment we received from management. Over the course of only three nights, we received three separate warning messages from administration threatening expulsion from the campsite due to “noise disturbances” and regulations we had absolutely no prior knowledge of. (skate without helmets)

For context: we were part of a larger group made up of seven different families from Australia, Japan, Brazil, Scotland, Italy, Portugal and Korea. Most of us had only just met at the campground itself. Naturally, the children connected immediately — riding bikes, skateboards, playing outdoors, laughing and socialising exactly as children should while camping.

There was no vandalism, no inappropriate language, and absolutely no disrespectful behaviour whatsoever. The children were simply excited to be outdoors, making friends, discovering a new environment and experiencing genuine social interaction away from screens and routine life.

As a Physical Education teacher with a PhD, I found this experience particularly confronting. Years of academic study and professional practice in child development, movement, social interaction and outdoor education strongly support the importance of exactly these kinds of experiences for children. Healthy play, spontaneous social connection, outdoor freedom and peer interaction are fundamental components of emotional, physical and cognitive development.

What we experienced at this campground went entirely against everything I have professionally learned and personally value regarding healthy childhood experiences and community interaction.

Instead of embracing a beautiful multicultural and family-oriented atmosphere, management made the entire experience feel uncomfortable, inappropriate and constantly monitored. What should have been a joyful and memorable holiday became tense and emotionally exhausting.

Sadly, we will never return.

I genuinely believe that without adaptation toward younger families and a more balanced approach to community living, this campground may struggle in the long term. The current customer base appears heavily dependent on an aging demographic, and without welcoming the next generation of campers and families, the future sustainability and longevity of the park may eventually decline.

Camping should represent freedom, nature, connection, cultural exchange and childhood memories — not fear of being removed for children laughing while playing outdoors. Grass areas were well maintained though.

Very disappointing experience 👎

Not a Family-Friendly Camping Experience

We experienced broken playground access, a sewage smell around the shower and kitchen areas, and poorly maintained facilities throughout our stay.

Unfortunately, the facilities were only part of the problem. Throughout our stay, we often felt watched and unfairly targeted over complaints and accusations that felt exaggerated or untrue.

What upset us most was the inconsistency in how rules were enforced. We were told to extinguish a fire immediately, and we complied straight away without argument. However, another younger group nearby continued having a fire and BBQ for hours without receiving any warning at all. Paying the same fees while being treated so differently felt unfair and discriminatory.

If we had known beforehand that this campground expected children to behave so quietly and restrictively, we would never have booked here. Families should be clearly informed about the type of environment and standards expected before arriving.

Camping should be about nature, connection, freedom, and children being able to enjoy the outdoors naturally — not families feeling anxious about constantly being monitored or criticised.

Sadly, we will not be returning.

Facilities were nasty. There was human waste in a bunch of the toilets. The showers were dirty. One of the ladies got mad at our kids for riding the bikes even though according to the rules it was allowed.

Since park has come under new management the maintenance has deteriorated.
We did arrive early at 11.00am and park entry time was 12.00pm soI can understand we couldn't get onto site straight away and we were told that grass had to be mowed.
When we returned at 12.30 site hadn't been mowed and grass was overgrown , then of course stayed that way for the next 3 weeks.
Ammenties were not as clean a previous years.
Young lady on the desk on the 17th April 2 days later was not interested when I told her that backpackers parked on street each night were coming in and using ammenties. Somehow they got the code.
Nice location and affordable caravan park. Quality amenities and helpful staff.
Camping superbe, installations de qualités, emplacement de rêve, franchement rien à redire on sent que c'est tenu par de vrais professionnels passionnés.

Had a tent site on the waterfront. An amazing view, location and the amenities were perfect.

Can’t wait to return 👌

We had such a great time at Noosa River. 3 families with 3 kids each. Kids could scoot around safely and lots of grass area down by the sandy river bank where they could play all day. Large sites and great amenities. We will definitely be back.

The park facilities were nice, it’s a big campground with lots of sites close together. The sites had water and power and a cement pad. There is a boat launch and a small beach area, likely bigger when the tide is out. It’s quite a ways from the main beach and town so not convenient in a motorhome if you don’t have other transportation.

Wij hadden helaas pech met het weer, maar de camping is goed onderhouden en heel mooi gelegen aan het water. Je kunt er barbecuën aan het water.

Vriendelijke mensen bij de receptie. Wifi werkt goed. Schone, moderne toiletten en douches.

Het stond helemaal vol en dat voelde wel wat druk, 's nachts was het echter stil.
Wow, this was an enjoyable stay.  Close to Noosa Heads, right on the river - where we swam.  Welcoming staff, lots of sites, peaceful, well kept gardens.  I’d happily stay here again.
Friendly professional staff and great facilities.
Clean and well run van park. We were very happy with the facilities and the park spacing between sites. Nicely grassed sites and raised concrete pads provided good drainage.

Super séjour dans ce camping !

Très bon accueil, la dame à la réception est adorable. Les équipements sont top, bien entretenus et tout est propre.

La vue est incroyable, c’est vraiment chouette de regarder le coucher de soleil en prenant l’apéro. L’ambiance est sympa, avec des gens de tous âges et de partout.

Je recommande 😊

Lovely position on the river!
Great location, close to shops , water , restaurants.
We were lucky to grab a booking . Facilities are clean and numerous..
It is very quiet considering how close the caravan park is to Noosa , Noosaville.
Great spot to bike , fish , swim or just chill.
Beautiful spot. Bring your boat.
Excellent camp spot overlooking the river, peaceful and with lots of activity on the water - could sit all day and never tire of it! Easy walk to Noosaville. Loved it!
A fantastic location, clean and well maintained and the staff are efficient and accommodating.
We both really enjoyed our stay  their especially our first time at  site looking over river front so nice .Thanks Paul & Robyn

Camping sensacional!
Super bem localizado em relação à cidade. Perto de tudo, além de estar praticamente dentro da água rs.
O Noosa River é delicioso e vale muito a pena.
A cozinha do camping tem a melhor vista possível.
Banheiros limpos e chuveiro bom.
Com certeza voltaria.
Noosa river holiday park, it truly is a holiday, quiet, lovely setting, clean amenities.  Ideal, we love it and return and return.

Absolutely gorgeous waterfront sites! I had a lovely stay, clean facilities, spots spaced out nicely. Not super private at the waterfront sites with guests playing next to and in front of the sites on the sand but that is to be expected. Was a bit awkward when other guests were sitting on the embankment less than a metre from my bed in my site (see photo—these are strangers haha) when there was plenty of space on the embankment in the park that didn’t have any campsites, but it was outside the little fence so nothing I could do/not on the holiday park to tell people that’s weird 😅

Regardless, will be returning :)

Well situated but packed like impossible with cars and boats and paddles and children and music. If you like action and people watching it’s the place. If you want space, calm it’s not. The amenities were ok at best but with that much people no wonder it’s not always perfect.
Beautiful white sandy river, very clean amenities, and friendly staff. Non powered beach sites are spacious.

Grea location, especially if you can book the unpowered waterfront sites. Toilets and kitchen are well maintained. Only call-out for me is the toilet gets really hot in summer. Would be good to install big industrial fans inside.
Lovely caravan park, right near the river.

We've stayed at this campsite twice. The first time was great, but the second time was not. Someone got the size of my caravan wrong and gave us a very small place. It wouldn't have been a problem, but the person working there didn't apologize; they just blamed me, saying they didn't make mistakes, which is ridiculous. We're all human and we all make mistakes, and as a customer, I'm always right. The rest of the staff was really nice and helpful!! The bathrooms were dirty and the grounds were neglected.
Awesome pizzas from "Fire and Wood pizza" there every Monday and Thursday.

I had a very unpleasant stay at this location. We were allocated to camp in a parking lot, the place was completely covered with leaves and dirt. But the worst part was that there was an AWFUL smell coming from the dump point that was next to us, so awful that we couldn’t even eat there without feeling sick.
The staff said that they couldn’t change us as they were fully booked, understandable. But I asked for a compensation, as I was paying 80 dollars per night to camp in such unpleasant and dirty smell, they offered me an ice cream instead. 😅
Just unbelievable.
Stay there a couple of times a year always great friendly staff clean amenities and sensational location
Sehr sauber und ordentlich, gut gelegen und ruhig
Lovely camp site, right on the river with nice walks and not far from restaurants etc. Free WiFi just didn't work - not the end of the world but would have been nice.
Fantastic location , great clean tidy park with with clean ammenities .
Absolute friendly and helpful staff.

We were camping at Noosa River Holiday Park this past weekend. The location is excellent however there were definitely some things that were lacking. We would visit again for sure but some small improvements could make the experience much better.

This time of year the park is at capacity and consequently the toilet/shower blocks needed more attention than they were receiving. EG. the soap dispenser was empty, paper towels ran out and toilets needed cleaning.

The campsites by the river, whilst having a really beautiful outlook, are fairly poor in comparison to the many other camp sites we’ve stayed at, including in the Noosa Shire. They are not on level ground so for those of us who camp a lot, we know how it is when you and your camp mattress keep slipping down a slope to the edge of the tent. Not ideal. There were also exposed trees roots which again make tenting a challenge (remember the fairytale of the princess and the pea - same principle with tree roots 😊). I think that these issues could be rectified fairly easily, perhaps with small timber retaining walls and sand to build up the sites. 🤷‍♀️

As I wrote above, we would definitely come back as the location is excellent and the river views beautiful. However feedback can be useful, especially if it is acted upon, so this feedback is offered in that spirit. 🙏

Great Park.
An easy pushbike ride to everything.
😎🤙
Heavenly spot
Love this place. Will difinately be back again.
Good and spacious place for caravans.  Clean amenities and the location is great 👍.  Hope to book again on our next caravanning.  Congratulations to the managements.
Beautiful spot & clean amenities.
Absolutely the loveliest place to camp. Clean facilities, great office staff, the most awesome camp kitchen by the river, quiet campers!

The spot the park is on is lovely. The receptionist however was awful on the 6/11 in the morning. The most bizarre interaction I’ve ever had with someone honestly. I’m not sure if she struggled to understand my grandad Birmingham accent or if she’d mistaken him for someone else but she honestly ruined our stay there for us which is such a shame as the lady we had on the evening of the 5th was the total opposite, super friendly and helpful.

We went in to ask for directions, my grandad said good morning and smiled and she didn’t seem happy to see him but she did say “how can I help”. To which he said “ these two have a question”. Referring to myself and my nan. Her reply was simply “what”. Now like I said maybe it was an accent barrier.

He repeated himself and then my nan proceeded to ask for directions. She did help us with this but then my grandad asked how far. She abruptly said “2k like I’ve already told you”. She had not! He replied “oh did you, I don’t remember”. My nan then jokingly said “don’t tell him anything, he never remembers”. His memory isn’t great so could be he had forgotten but I didn’t hear her say either and neither did my nan. So whether she thought she’s spoken to him before even though she hadn’t. I don’t know.

She then turned and said “I don’t like the way you talk to women”. I thought I’d misheard her or she was being sarcastic. so I laughed. She then proceeded to say “ you’re making me uncomfortable so I’m don’t with the conversation”. We were so confused that we just said okay and left. It wasn’t until I’d left that it hit me on how unprofessional and rude the interaction was.

Now I completely understand people being uncomfortable in certain situations and I’m a massive advocate for women in those situations but it’s not like she was dealing with 10 people at once or something that may have made her flustered and also what about making men uncomfortable. That ordeal left my grandad very uncomfortable also. He’s had to have two big operations this year to remove cancer and we left that conversation and the whole way to noosa he was asking me “ what he did wrong “ and “did she think I’m sexist?” Because he knows any time he says anything even slightly out of pocked I’m the first one to tell him but nothing my grandad said could have been taken in a nasty way so I’m still all these days later left so confused. I’ve then looked at your reviews and it doesn’t seem to be the first time and if I’d had to take a guess I’d assume the woman other people are complaining about her also.

I hope you can address these issues because the holiday park itself is in a nice location and has so much potential. She ruined it for us and I’m sure for many other people to.

We were staying on a powered campsite, the power was further away than many other sites we have stayed on and the water was too far away so we were unable to refill our campervan. Amenities were clean.
Always love this place,very hard to get into at times,
Great park, good to be back again
Bagni con il codice che se non lo ricordi fai fatica ad entrare, cucina con i fuochi al vento dove è impossibile cucinare pure una semplice pasta per il  vento e fuochi troppi piccoli
Fantastic location right on the river and so close to excellent cafes, restaurants and ferry to Hastings street. Facilities were very clean and staff super friendly .
Visited friends that were staying there
Great park, perfect location. Often difficult to get a booking because of its popularity, but we did manage two gorgeous days there.

Lovely location- shame about the free campers who inhabit the end of Russell street each night. Car doors open and close till 1 am and there is a white old van who has about 8  visitors each night who drive into Russell street and leave again almost immediately. I think the council should police the no camping rule.
Great location easy access to everywhere

One of the best located caravan parks in Queensland.  Safe little beach, boat ramp for the tinny, good fishing 🎣 , 5 * restaurants and shops within walking distance, food trucks pop in a few nights a week, the coffee man drives through about 8am each morning and of course sundowner drinks with fellow campers, see sunset photo! We have been coming here annually for many years, twice a year, and have made lots of wonderful friends! Great for grey nomads and families! Thank you Kourtney and Team for looking after us!
Best spot ever and Courney in the office so lovely and so helpful

Another noosa council scam! Tent sites are a disgrace for the price (woodchip parking space)
They purposefully wont answer phone calls.
Shame on Noosa council for allowing their campsites be run like this.

Location of the camp is great, Noosa is fabulous.
However;
There are only two bathrooms facilities and one kitchen, for a looooot of guests.
If you are unlucky like us, we ended up having to drive to the kitchen as if we forgot a single item it's take about 15 minutes to return. It does have a nice view as a positive note. But it is not conveniently positioned. In general we found the camp to lacks on services and amenities. Also, no possibility of paying by card for the laundry machines. Compared to other camps definitely on the lower end.
Great park friendly staff, clean amenities and close to cafes and eateries

Had such a great stay at Noosa River Holiday Park! The park is clean, well kept and in a perfect spot with great beach access. A special shout-out to Courtney who was absolutely amazing, and to Maureen for fitting us in on our first night. Courtney was super friendly, helpful and went out of her way to make sure we were looked after, not only in extending our stay, but also on the daily. Little things like that really made the time there feel easy and relaxed. Definitely will be back!
Been coming to this park for 15 years- great location, great people, great facilities. Close to shops and restaurants, and what a beautiful river

Paid $67 a night for an unpowered tent site.
I moved to a different park and got a powered van site for $55 a night.
Great place to stay .staff really friendly and Helpful. Met so many nice people at that Park thumbs up
Loved the simplicity and how quiet the park is. The most perfect spot by the river. Can't wait for my next stay.
We had a great 2 night stay on a waterfront tent site. The amenities were clean and location was great. A half an hour walk to the shops/main beach. Highly recommend!

What a great park! My family come here every Easter for 10 days and I have decided to join along and a new tradition it is. Facilities are roomy and as clean as you expect them to be at a holiday park. The most perfect location with the river at your door step. Only a short stroll to all the river has to offer. The usual people will park in your allocated car spots which can be annoying and reception isn’t always easy to get a hold of. Overall this is a great place and I highly recommend.

Danke, dass wir so kurzfristig noch einen Platz für eine Nacht bekommen haben. Wir hatten eine schöne Zeit,der Platz war sehr gut und nach Noosa rein ( Strand/Shopping) läuft man 25min. Es geht auch ein Bus.

Great location, but unfortunately my experience at reception was disappointing. The young lady working there was surprisingly rude—not only to me, but also to an older gentleman ahead of me in line. At first, I assumed he might have been at fault, but when I received the same treatment, it was clear this wasn’t an isolated incident. I understand everyone can have a bad day, but for the price I paid to stay here, the experience really fell short
Clean park and amenities. Well presented especially the double concrete slabs for caravan sites. Must book well in advance though.
Love it
Amazing location right on the water. Bathrooms are super clean!! Great facilities
Beautiful park , located in an amazing place. Quiet at night and close to Noosa Heads
Love it here at Noosaville, walking distance to restaurants etc, the caravan park is neat & tidy, a good amount of shower/toilet blocks and clean, & a great camp kitchen with a view
Good site with nice clean facilities
Such a lovely place for a winter getaway. The park is well maintained and has a lovely sheltered location on the river. Sunset viewing from the riverbank is a must!

What a great place . We will be booking in here soon again  . Close to everything,  & bonus pizza van , & Thia takeaway & fish market van calling in & setting up . Also BBQ Areas for everyone to use .. well we are back here again . So love this caravan park .
Perfect spot on the Noosa river for a  tranquil break.
Perfect camp spot and reasonably priced. Facilities are very good and all clean. Views are excellent.

Noosa River Holiday Park is my favourite place to stay in South East Queensland.
I have stayed  there 9 times and looking forward to my next visit.
The location is fantastic. The facilities first class and the staff are very professional, friendly  and helpful.
Loved this place. Quiet and yet so close to everything. Will definitely be returning.
Great location. Clean amenities

The environment is lovely, in front of Noosa river, it’s quiet. There laundry and dryer
But the commun area are dirty, for the price it’s a shame. The door of the bathroom were like this for the 3 days, the bins are always full, some day there is no soap to wash your hands
The kitchen it’s not really clean, like the floor and tables are sticky
We’ve paid 188$ for 3 night for a power campsite

One of the best parks we have ever stayed at. Great amenities, fantastic pools and nice customer service. Loved our stay here and would highly recommend it. Worth the extra money you'll have to spend as it is more pricey than other parks
Well priced so close to hasting st. Great place to Sup or kayak from.  Close to river bank. Not a romantic or quiet location,  but what holiday park is 🤣
Great stay wonderful location very peaceful
Fabulous location
Lovely staff, quiet, amenities are very clean and everything you need, beautiful view of the river.
Really good camping site with brilliant facilities. Would highly recommend.

Avoid this place. They do not worry about the no noise after 10pm policy. There is no on site after hours caretaker of such a busy park. The after hours number goes to a message bank. Only after repeated calls was it answered with  sleepy Hello in Sydney. And nothing they could do from there. Caretaker eventually turned up at 11.30pm, quietened them down but the party continued to 3am!
Morning staff could not care, glib answers, quoting policy, had to refer it on, for due consideration by management
The most expensive park in 6000km and the least desirable
Well worth a visit staff are really helpful and friendly great vibe all the amenities  very well maintained
Très bon camping, nous y sommes rester deux nuits, douches et toilettes très propres, laundry a disposition, proche de tout.

Serenity from 4-6 pm. Amenities +++! However, noise from boats via public boat ramp and no boom gate access meant campers could come and go whenever they pleased. Curfew (10pm - 7 am) could not be enforced.
Fantastic park and wonderful amenities.

I arrived  Early for my reservation young lady called summer could not have been more helpful in every way instead of moving full times. She text me back and said she had organise just one extra move instead of four.
Facilities were clean and tidy, camp spots were amazing, staff were friendly. Will be back!!!

Not worth it, camping tent spots are too small, overcrowded, they keep sending you messages about noise and kids like you’ve never read the fine print, just because new neighbors moved in on our last two days, they’re older folks and complain about the littlest things, they keep sending messages like the park is not for everyone to share, didn’t even make much noise just basically talking and having a good time, no parties kinda noise.  We are suppose to just breath and don’t make noise, then threatens that we will be barred from the park if it keeps going, didn’t even bother to come down and see what the neighbors was complaining about…. Sorry but we are not coming back and this was by far one of the worst camp grounds we been to, we could’ve retaliated but we don’t do that, we are there to relax and not bother anyone, I mean if older folks actually wants peace and extra quite, just stay home or go somewhere there’s no one, think of others too, be realistic that there will be more people than being at home.

We have stayed here quite a few times and despite it being an incredible location and great families and guests who come and stay, the customer service lets us down every time. The service can be rude, dismissive, extremely unhelpful and lacks any kind of compassion or solutions for simple questions and queries. I’ve witnessed the service on the desk be rude about a very reasonable and rational request by another guest by Courtney. I avoid reception at all costs when she’s working the desk. Shocking representation for the park and not a good asset for Noosa tourism, when it has the bones to be something special! Shout out to Sheree who is the only one I’ve had pleasant and helpful conversations with. A pleasure to chat with! Thank you Sheree!
Great location, clean facilities and friendly staff. Brilliant

Great location near river, easy walk into Noosaville restaurants ,
very clean amenities would of loved to have stayed longer so will return
Good campground - bad weather - (very) wet season. Amenities ok, clean. Beach only Noosa River.
Immaculately maintained park. Fantastic location with river frontage, including own beach and close to all Noosa has to offer.
Very nice campsite. Great view on the water!

Amazing spot kids loved it
The only complaint is regarding online booking never find available dates better contact by phone

Absolutely lovely location with great faclities.
Double slab sites, beautiful surrounds and gardens. The staff really know how to look after you, great people. Right on the river with a beach. A short walk to Noosa Heads.
Loved our stay so much we booked for next year. 10 out of 10.
NASTY receptionist. We went elsewhere. A shame management aren't addressing this issue as it appears in alot of your posts.

Our stay was very enjoyable, an excellent holiday park with clean amenities and grounds, very large clean slabs on the Noosa river.
We look forward in revisiting very soon.

WiFi needs sorting. Had to use our data costing us more. We pay for this at powered sites.
Site itself was nice

Location is great and the view of the public kitchen area is amazing.

WiFi is the worst, cleaning could be improved. Tables in the kitchen are disgustingly sticky. Former reviews reflected this issue already but seems not to be improved. There is nothing offered for kids.
Always typing in a code at the toilet is just annoying especially with little kids.

Price / Value leads me to the feedback of 2 stars.
They set us directly next to the toilets which was also not cool. Half of the campground is empty and we kindly asked if we could get a spot next to the neighborhood gate that our kids are closer to the playground.
They wanted to get more money from us for the same spot and setting and just 15 m away from our defined spot.

There are surely better campgrounds nearby.
Fantastic place to be even in a storm love it there.👍
Incredible park with fantastic staff
Bathrooms were great, sites were a good size, and there was a great little beach 50 feet from our campsite.

The positive...Spent a great few days camping waterfront in my tent.
Crystal clear water to swim in 10 mts from my tent.
Walked just 20mts along the beach, to use the well equipped camp kitchen a few times a day.
The not so positive...Tables in the camp kitchen could do with a good scrub, were quite sticky with...??? !!
Thank you for having me...will be back soon ☺
Nice place to stay. Friendly and accommodating staff. Clean amenities

Really nice stay, only negatives were that we were placed on a slope with a rooftop tent so we couldn't sleep without feeling like we were sliding down a hill. Also the front office didn't have any basic supplies for purchase.
Great camping by the river. Good amenities. Nice and quiet
Park has everything you need all the facilities were clean and better than expected  . Would recommend to anybody looking for a great place to stay

This was honestly the worst park we’ve ever stayed at. The facilities were dirty and gross. The camp kitchen was filthy, the eating tables were so sticky and cleaning involved wiping down the counter, but only sweeping the floors (not even underneath the cupboards, where it was obviously dirty). The toilets was a whole other story. We arrived in the afternoon, so we brushed the dirty bathroom stalls/toilets off as something that happened after cleaning, but no. It stayed like that until we checked out almost two days later. Besides that the toilet amenities just feels like one big public toilet - it was dark and grey-ish lighting and nowhere to place your bags or toiletries. The park itself is completely cramped and doesn’t feel like a nice place. It has nothing to do with the size of the park, since we’re currently at a bigger park, but the space itself didn’t feel welcoming. It was also the first campsite where we didn’t feel safe leaving the trunk of our van open (we have a drying rack there). We would definitely NOT go again - much rather stay a bit more outside of Noosa heads or even paying much more to stay in a hotel room of sorts. Definitely not worth 61 dollars per night.

We stayed for a few days at an unpowered tent site. The location is beautiful and it’s amazing to wake up by the water. Would love to come back! The facilities were generally in good condition. Unfortunately, we found part of the cleaning staff a bit unfriendly in their approach to customers, however, the ladies at reception were always helpful and polite.
Thanks for the lovely stay!

The view, the amenities, and the general environment are great.
However, the tent site is the worst one we have ever had.
We booked an unpowered tent site and all we got was a small block of area, which 1/4 of space was garden bed, and 1/4 of space is too uneven to put anything on.
The worst part is, the ground was so hard that we could barely peg anything in. Some parts of the land were just rocks (or concrete) 3cm under the soil.
Admin girl was also very rude and unhelpful. Considering this is not a cheap camp site, and we don’t get to choose our spot beforehand, this type of block should not be sold as a camping spot.
Absolutely enjoyed our camping experience at our unpowered site 5m from beach front. Cute ducks walking around sites. Clean facilities and friendly staff would highly recommend for all ages
Great location, loved the water views

We had booked two consecutive nights on waterfront sites. On Day 2, we returned to the park to find another group occupying our site. We went to reception and were informed that our site had been allocated to another party. While mistakes happen, the solution offered - a non-waterfront site at the back of the park - was unsatisfactory. What followed was an experience of customer service that I can only describe as the worst I have ever encountered. While the receptionist was empathetic and apologetic, the manager was extremely rude and entirely dismissive of our concerns. She condescendingly said, “It’s not your site, it’s ours.” It left us feeling extremely upset and ruined what should have been a peaceful evening on our holiday.
Great park
Lovely location beside the river and very pleasant grounds with clean amenities. 10 minute walk to beautiful parklands and numerous eateries along Gympie Tce.

Unfortunately our latest experience at this park was not a positive one, non-guests are increasingly using the toilet and shower facilities and we did not feel safe at night as there is no security and poor lighting as a result of power issues. We also noticed that gardens and lawns were overgrown and not taken care of as they have been in the past.

We had a good stay here over the Christmas break. It was disappointing that some items (including our towels) were taken on Christmas Eve from the washing line so just be careful if leaving items out (it's just luck of the draw). Our site was hidden in the corner with a good amount of shade, amenities were great with laundry, camp kitchen outside and hot showers. Would recommend 😁
Wonderful plaxe to spend a few days...lots to do and see..fine dining and shopping close by. Free busses...Fabulous

Lovely park with a great location right on the river. Really good amenities close to all the sites. Couple of issues for feedback which prevented me from giving 5 stars:
The fridge in the camp kitchen needs replacing. It constantly leaks on all the food, so you need to put your things in a container with a lid, and the fridge water pools at the bottom so our things were literally swimming - quite unhygienic and off-putting.  We tried to keep it clean by mopping up the water, and others were doing the same, but it is an old fridge and with so many people using it, it needs to be upgraded to a proper commercial fridge.
The other issue was with insects. We usually dont worry about the odd cockroach, ants etc as that's what camping is all about but the amount of ants and shield bugs (we googled them and think they are called "Brown marmorated stink bugs") was really bad. We informed the staff, and the guy that came and looked at our camper walls & roof covered with shield bugs was quite surprised himself. The ground was literally teaming with ants - we bought some spray and sprayed everywhere, but it appears they have gone up the legs of our camper and into our tow bar, and when we got home we are finding nests of ants in all sorts of places in our camper. We really spent time sweeping the site, and getting rid of all the ants we could see but they have got into the nooks and crannys of our camper and have unfortunately have come home with us.  So the area really needs to be urgently treated.
However we would definitely return but stay in a different part of the park, which hopefully has a normal amount of insects.

We have visited this holiday park several times over the past nine years, drawn by its stunning location and previously impeccable standards. Unfortunately, during our most recent stay, we noticed a significant decline in management care and attention. The communal kitchen was unclean on multiple occasions, which was disappointing given its previous standards. Additionally, the power points were out of service for nearly 24 hours, which was inconvenient. Another concern was the lack of hand soap in the showers, which went unaddressed for several days until I mentioned it to reception. While they did place a single household soap dispenser afterward, it quickly ran out, suggesting it wasn’t adequately monitored. It’s disheartening to see such a wonderful park not being maintained to the high standards it once upheld. Of the four customer service representatives I interacted with, two were noticeably rude and seemed to lack basic hospitality skills. This was disheartening, as friendly and helpful staff are such an important part of the guest experience. We hope to see improvements in the future.
Had great time
Great place!

My wife and I stayed at the Noosa River Holiday Park for 6 days in November to December 24. We have stayed before, facilities are excellent, location gives easy access to Hastings St and/or Noosaville either via bike or by ferry. Highly recommended.
Robert Zigterman

My family and I have been coming here for 5 years now in early Dec. the last 2 years we have noticed a big change in the business model. The people that have been coming  to this caravan park spans generations. it’s a shame it’s going down hill with the new management company.  The  reception staff are absolutely horrible very rare to get a smile seems to depend on what side of the bed they get out on. There just isn’t any care shown anymore, the workers are just there to collect their pay checks and basically don’t want to be there. It’s just lucky the location makes up for everything else.  It seems as the Council just picked the lowest management company in the tender process and didn’t  really care about other values. typical.
Modern and clean facilites. Friendly staff

Schöner Campingplatz
Schöne Aussicht auf den Noosa River
Zu Empfehlen!

Very nice campsite.
Our spot was right by the water with a great view.
The city is quickly accessible by car. Unfortunately, we only stayed for one night.
I can 100% recommend this campsite.

Noosa River Holiday Park is in a great, shady location on the river & perfect for fishing, swimming & water sports. It is in walking distance to great cafes & restaurant. The amenities are kept clean & ample for the size of the park.
Best holiday
Don’t like providing a review as rebooking will become more difficult at this great location with very helpful management. Thank you team!
Great location, clean facilities and very quite. Loved it!
Great caravan park perfectly positioned on Noosa river where you at a 15mins walk to Noosaville,
Great location! Located on the nice calm beach of Noosa river and only a short drive from the main beach and Noosa. Facilities were superb

Great location right on the river with oversized well maintained caravan sites with plenty of room. Very quiet at night.  Our only complaint was that it appeared the amenities were not cleaned or at least not cleaned very well our entire stay. Snot and hair on the shower wall all week. Dirty toilets with same stains not cleaned all week. Seems office staff have little idea on facilities/ ground staff duties having been undertaken or completed.
Otherwise enjoyable.

Although the facilities were great and spacious, they placed me just next to the kitchen, which was really close and I could smell everything all day while someone else was cooking, not to mention the noise at night. My suggestion: having free spaces around common areas such as bathrooms and kitchen.

This is a great caravan park. Right on the river. There are big caravan spot. The toilet/shower are amazing. The only thing if you’re tall the shower heads are low. Close to everything you want need. Good walking/bike riding so close. And not sure why. But we stay 2 nites and the no vacations sign was up. It was not full at all.
Great spot
The staff were amazing they sorted everything out for me and my while family . Every year we go to noosa river caravan park they make booking and staying there a stress free experience so thankyou .
Fantastic location on the Noosa River, good facilities, well run with a range of camping sites. Walking distance to Weyba Tce Noosaville cafes and restaurants.

I would say this campsite really packs people in, it's very squished and you are super close to your neighbours.
The facilities were really good and the reception team were kind and helpful.
Good location with water front.

A lovely park on the Noosa River, we have stayed here before, we arrived this time on a Sunday, as you can’t check in before 12.00 we got there just after, the office was closed, we waited 45 mins before someone opened the door. We shouldn’t have had to wait, by the time you check in & set up most of the afternoon is gone, most places  check in is at 11.00 which gives you extra time to enjoy the park. They sent me a welcome email the day before with the park rules, with that email they could have added our site number so we could have set up while the office was shut & then gone back to the office to settle our account.  Not impressed !

Overall the camping is quite nice with clean sanitary services (unless showers are only at 1m70 height), well equipped kitchen and nice location.

Nevertheless, the price (>$50/night) for the unpowered sites is too high for what you get. The provided camping places are not flat at all, hence, necessitating you to sleep on an unbalanced area. Besides this, it’s not attractable to eat/to have apero outside since the turkeys and ducks immediately attack you for food. The reception of the camping was not able to provide information about what to do/recommend in the surrounding area or national park. This should definitely be some points of attention for the future.
Beautiful spot, lovely park, great facilities
Top
Great location, clean modern amenities.
Right on the river and within walking distance to fabulous restaurants and cafes. Amenities are excellent. A great place to stay

We enjoyed a wonderful week at the NRH Park. Beautiful park, river views and hinterland at sunset were stunning. Exceptionally well maintained and loads of room on our site. We plan to holiday here again and again! Chris and Diana Anwyl

Very nice camping, not cheap for a parking spot with electricity (190$ for 3 nights), the amenities were super clean, hot water all the time, and the kitchen area is well made with many cooking stations both bbqs and electrical heating. The location is great, you can walk to both Noosa town and heads and the river view is lovely for sunset.

It is a very nice park situated right on the Noosa river. Close to restaurants and Hastings St is only about a 15 minute walk
Great place for happy hr

We just left from our first stay at NRHP.
This is a fantastic park. really good amenities and friendly helpful staff….we are  hoping to have a longer stay here again next year.
Great park in middle of Noosaville. Fantastic range of restaurants and clubs. Staff friendly amenities spotless great camp kitchen excellent for campers.  Hassle free checkin
Very nice place to stay in. Great reception staff, very helpful. New amenities. Located on Noosa Riverbanks, close to shops and strip of cafes. Walking distance to Noosa Head main beach and shops.

We had a wonderful stay recently. The reception staff , in particular the night time office lady , was so helpful and friendly, we appreciated her service. We had a great site , the amenities were excellent.
We don’t always love caravan parks these days but I definitely wanted to give this park a great review.

We have a roof top tent so dont require much room. We rung up and spoke to reception on the phone to book and advised me have a rooftop as well. These tent sites are old overflow parking spots which they have thrown wood chips over. Each camp-site was no bigger than a disabled car spot but on a slop and wasn't even enough room for us to even put out ladder out!
When we went up to explain we couldn't set up camp we were meet with the worst customer service in ANY camp site around Australia! We were told they would 'apply for a refund' followed by an eye roll from the reception staff!!!. For $56 a night AVOID this place!!!!!!!!!

Flat sites, clean amenities, normally a good rating… but sadly I had to deal with the lady at the reception because of some other rude campers. And that wasn’t a pleasure. No understanding for my complaint, no sorry for the issue and telling me it would be my fault

Caravan park is on the river, great place
spacious sites for caravans, good amenities.
Sadly the park is lacking in clothes lines space for the number guests staying.

Very nice camping, some beautiful unpowered spots with water views. Perfect to watch the sunset.
Clean showers and toilets.
The only minor thing were the boats passing on the river from early  very early in the morning.

Have been coming here with our 3 x families for over 10 years.
Noosa Council have changed the management tender over to the  new managers 'Noosa Holiday Parks' and since then the toilets have been less clean, the communal kitchen also less clean.
We tried to book again this year and the communication was terrible with phones never answered, and the dates were incorrect and when approached about the error, it was not resolved. This error was not only our booking but others as well. Also withholding $30 booking fee after their error of booking incorrect dates and cancelling 6 x months prior to the booking (no other park does this!). Good luck dealing with the new incompetency of 'Noosa Holiday Parks'. Very poor.
2024-02-01 - We were here with three kids and there was nothing to complain about, but there was also not anything extraordinary.

The park is in a great location and beautiful to sit by the river in the afternoon. The park definitely needs some updating and unfortunately the amenities weren’t clean even after the sign had been out saying they were undergoing a “deep clean”. Washing machines were mouldy. With some upgrades and a proper clean it would be much better.

We camped here and found it to be very clean and a great place to stay. It is situated on the waterfront and has a little beach area with pristine white sand and pelicans wading nearby. It is only a 7 minute drive to the centre of Noosa from here. We will stay here again.

Enjoyed just a 2 night stay recently in this well located park near Noosa. We could walk to Noosaville shops and rode our bikes to Noosa itself taking in Hastings Street, The National Park and Noosa junction. So much easier to ride ( in bike lanes) than try drive and find parking.

Great Park but   the staff need training in customer service. I don't appreciate being treated like an annoyance by officious high handed dismissive staff. It
is offensive.

One of the reception staff has a really difficult attitude and made it hard to check in and get set up. Not sure whats going on there, the rest of the staff seem helpful so it probably comes down to who you get.

And make sure you ask questions before paying any addtional costs at check-in. They seem to be in the habit of "accidentally " requesting more money...

Don’t do your laundry here. All my clothes were still dirty and my blanket was burned when I got it out of the dryer. No apologies from the blond lady at the desk or compassion. She looked like she was on something. My first very weird Aussie experience.
Another top seaside spot, one of the very best!
Such a lovely park to stay in, the sites are huge, the location is great, everything is kept well and the staff are wonderful.
Great little find.

Perfect location with a nice beach at the river. Site was fully booked on our first night in Noosa Heads so we had to find someplace else, second day we had a spot:) convenient and modern amenities buildings

Super clean and convenient holiday park. Great unpowered sites right on the beach where there’s vibrant boat and birdlife - pelicans, cranes and herons galore. Great kitchen facilities and a fun community spirit. Wished we’d stayed longer.

Ein absoluter Wohlfühlort für uns. Wir waren zweimal hier und haben uns immer wohl gefühlt. Schön ist es abends, gefühlt geht der ganze Campingplatz zum Flussufer und schaut der Sonne beim untergehen zu. Wir kommen gerne wieder und empfehlen diesen Ort gern weiter.

Had a rude greeting at front desk from the Lady on Friday at about 2pm.

I felt gaslit when l was being friendly, l don't know why l had this greeting, perhaps she is unhappy in her job. Customer service goes a long way and l certainly didn't get this.

I've never experienced meeting someone like this before.

When l told my husband, l looked at the reviews and it looks as though this is common feedback.

It's also worth noting the grounds are very open, anyone can drive in if this is something that matters to picking a caravan park.

I won't be going back or recommending.

We had there a stay of 4 nights and stayed at the campsite. The place was wonderful and the facilities clean. Only the birds in the kitchen were annoying as well as people who made some loud noise in the kitchen after 11 pm.

We love our Easter break here, relaxing, swimming, shopping, great cafes & restaurants locally…….meeting up with friends year after year.
Facilities are excellent, with lovely staff!!
What a fantastic Holiday Park! The lake, the amenities, the staff everything was great! We only stayed one night but we throughly enjoyed it, best campsite I've stayed in, in Noosa!

It was a first time of us. Really good location and all amenities are good. Bathroom cleaned, spent one night. Awesome night.it good for both tent and carvan. 3p tent with car and powered what else we need4😅. We'll definitely comeback again soon.

Stopped in for a one night stay, great facilities and location was spot on. Although would've appreciated better service from the receptionist when checking in. Only the location of our camp site was explained and then handed the map. Wasn't an overly friendly or welcoming interaction upon checking in but otherwise had a nice time.

*Bathrooms need to be cleaned more often.
*5 out of  5 dryers were not working.
*Debris along the walkway alongside the amenities needs to be swept or blower cleaned each day.  It was very dirty.

Other than that it's a great park.

One of the best located caravan parks in Queensland. It was our first time going there and it’s a little hidden place and it’s just so beautiful and relaxing and it has a Safe little beach, boat ramp for kayaks I can’t wait to come again when it’s not raining but was still a lot of fun and the toilets and showers are amazing some photos from our little trip

The camping is really cute with a beautiful view on the river.
We were working during our stay so maybe the cleaning in the bathroom at 8/9 o'Clock was not the best moment. Oserwise, everything was perfect.

Стандартный Кемп для Австралии, большая кухня с красивым видом, пеликаны. Чистые душевые. Есть прачечная с сушильными машинами и большими раковинами для стирки. Есть беседки и гриль зоны на территории, пляж и можно купаться и рыбачить. Из минусов в местах для палаток нет газона. На кухне работала только одна плита и один гриль. Для такого большого Кемпа этого очень мало. За неделю моего прибывания так и не починили не работающие плиты. Остальное всё превосходно. Цена стандартная, но считаю , что за место для маленькой палатки завышено.

We booked a caravan site Nov 23 and expected that pre booking 4 months in advance the site given would have been better than the second last van site (72) from the river and with a view of the amenties block and rubbish bin smell. If we were ever to book here again we would endeavour to have a better position within the park. At least the amenities block (adjoining site 72) could have a screen to block persons accessing, asking for the access code (as some guests forget).

Wirklich ein wunderschöner Campingplatz. Die Lage ist 5 * wert. Auch die Sauberkeit der Waschräume ist sehr gut. Die Freundlichkeit der Mitarbeiter ist ausbaufähig. Wir hatten 2 Nächte gebucht, wollten aber bereits eine Nacht früher anreisen. Telefonisch haben wir niemanden erreichen können, per Mail hat niemand geantwortet.
Als wir dort waren hing ein Schild, dass es keine freien Plätze mehr gibt. Hatten aber Glück und bekamen einen. Auf unsere Nachfrage, dass wir ja eine Mail geschrieben haben, wurde gesagt, dass dies nicht sein könne und uns wurde der Türcode für die Waschräume nur für einen Tag gegeben. Nachts stand mein Mann dann vor der Toilette und kam nicht rein, weil der Code nicht mehr gültig war. Am nächsten Tag gab es daraufhin dann die Auskunft, ja ist ja klar, sie haben ja den Stellplatz gewechselt. Also kinderfreundlich ist das nicht. Daher 1* Abzug.
Servizi igienici ottimi e posizione bellissima vicino al fiume

La ubicación del Holiday Park es excelente, frente a una playita en el río de Noosa.
Sin embargo, la cocina del camping no está preparada para la cantidad de gente que albergan. Había solamente UNA hornalla para cocinar!! Tuvimos que hacer cola por más de una hora para poder usar la única hornalla que funcionaba.

We must say that we were very pleased with the customer service and the assistance of the staff even late in the evening when we encounter some challenges. Initially when we arrived we did not get the camping site  that we were told we were going to get,  however, the staff was super helpful to allocate us and accommodate us. Then one night when we came back with our campervan, our site was taken by someone else who put up a tent on our site, this was quite late in the evening, and we had to go to the accommodation of the local caretakers, and they were very kind to come to to ask the ones who camped in that spot to remove their tent and move to their appropriate spot where they were supposed to be, and then we could again park our campervan where we were the previous night.  The staff was more than happy and willing to assist us with this even after hours which we very very much appreciated. So certainly want to thank them for their work and assistance.

Amazing place
Loved it

In general, we had a great time of camping in Noosa River Holiday Park. Toilet & bathroom is clean.
However, there are some things in booking process that need to be improved. I hope this feedback is helpful.

+ Booking online does not allow to book 2 sites next together.
+ Calling staff to book was not helpful at all because they gave me 2 not-closed-together sites on check-in date without telling us in advance.
+ Emailing was very slow to response. It seems that they replied email each week by different staff, so the email context lost.
+ Deposit amount was not taken into account on check-in date ( one staff told me they already deducted the deposit amount). Then we realised the issue, we had to talk with another friendly staff on next day to ask refund. Luckily, the refund process is very quick and smooth.

We will camp here again in future, but we would book only one site so the process will be simpler.
Great spot

Great campsite and facilities are extremely clean. Unfortunately the staff leave very early so its impossible to book and get door code for showers & toilets. Says there's no availability, but place was more or less empty. When we finally got in touch with a staff member at reception, they were exceptionally rude!! Great place, maybe you need to train your staff up on better communication skills! And maybe not finish hours early?!?

Have stayed at NRHP for several years now.  It used to be hard to book but worth it.  Looks like the management changed over sometime in the past 12 months.  I have meet two young girls (probably backpackers) at the reception.  Both were officious (not to be confused with efficient) and bordering on outright rude.  The park is looking like it needs some love.  Maybe the previous managers were just great at their job, maybe the current manager is just terrible - probably a bit of both -  but the difference is VERY obvious.  My main reason for raising a review is that the code to the bathrooms we received on arrival did not work.  When we checked with the reception they said others had had the same problem so they gave us the "general" code which would definitely work.  Which it did for 3 days then it didn't.  I raised this with the reception (again officious) and was given a third code.  She also mentioned that the second code we got was a "general" code and should work.  The third code works (for the moment at least) but if it fails in the middle of the night, I will be tempted to leave a deposit at the door to reception.  The park is lovely and in the best location.  It is a real shame to see it go downhill slowly and the reception experience to be so unnecessarily rude (from both staff I have had the unfortunate experience of meeting).  I believe the managers need to have a good look at what they are doing and how they are doing it.  I also notice that the park is not nearly as full as it has been previously.  I understand why.

Stayed 5 nights and loved it, great fishing, walking distance to Hastings street or Gympie Terrace restaurants. Clean park top location, large spots. You can fit car, van and boat easily on most spots.

The kitchen was extremely dirty and basically we felt unsafe to use it. I have heard from returning campers that there was an ownership change last year, not sure if it's true but apparently the kitchen was spotless before. I really hope they will hire a professional cleaner as otherwise I wouldn't return. Many things broke during our stay in the kitchen/ bathroom area and nothing was fixed either. They only put up a sign, that says out of order - that's it. We never seen a maintenance guy or anybody attempting to repair. The fridge broke down 2 days overnight during our stay and spoilt all food in there. The park is really understaffed, I think the owners are trying to cut costs which is a shame. The holiday park is really great location and really quiet at night. I am really hoping they fix this as it's a great spot.

We booked ‘premium riverside unpowered tent sites’.  The sites were dirt only, no grass. They were so unkept that they attracted pigeons. I attempted to speak to one of the office ladies, she replied with “I don’t care if you don’t like it, I received a complaint that you started your car at 6am this morning and the diesel fumes went into another tent”. I replied that it was our friends car and their battery had ran out, they started the car to drive off and recharge it. She was really rude and u pleasant to deal with. There were a lot of other travellers in the unpowered sites that relied on solar power too.

Also, we stayed there for 2 nights, the bathrooms were not cleaned whilst we were there.

It was a horrible experience and I will not return. I recommend this park for caravans only, it is not suitable for tents

They booked me on another campsite and when I got there they didnt have any availability so they told me they would refund the money because they knew it was their mistake. They never refunded me and are not answering my emails.
Bad🤮 honestly so many bugs in there and unfixable problems, man you are so useless, no wonder why Toowoomba is so much better then you

Lors de notre road trip sur la côte est, nous nous sommes arrêtés à Noosa le 2 décembre et cherchions un endroit où passer la nuit. Nous dormions dans notre voiture, nous n’avions donc pas besoin d’un espace caravane, une simple place de parking nous aurait suffi. Nous demandons donc à la dame à l’accueil s’il était toujours possible de dormir dans le Holiday Park, même sur une simple place de parking. Celle-ci nous a répondu de manière très froide et désagréable que ce n’était pas possible. Pas de problème si c’est indiqué de manière polie, mais elle n’a même pas daigné nous regarder dans les yeux.
No place to put the caravan drainage hose on these three sites quite unhealthy to see drainage just running down the sites . We were next to the camp kitchen on the river.

Our second stay at this great caravan park. It’s in a perfect spot right on the river close to everything Noosa has to offer. We had a double slab with plenty of room to park in front of van. Amenities were clean and plentiful.   My only negative comments, which management should address are:
1. Ghost sites: People have come to this camp for 20+ years and can rebook as first priority. The number of prime sites at this sought after property with nobody present for weeks on end is disgraceful and unfair. They pay but don’t stay. Our neighbour went to Europe for a month and left his van there.
2. Regulars with two cars, two boats plus their accommodation on their site overcrowds the place.
3. Kids hooning around the walkways and around amenities at speed on their bikes and scooters is dangerous and very disruptive.
Otherwise it’s great if you can get a booking.

We have been coming to the park in Nov Dec for 4 weeks for 18 years, this year the state of cleanliness of the bathrooms was shocking,  get the old cleaners back, it seems to have dropped standards since the new management has taken over

We have been camping at Noosa River Holiday Park for 7 or 8 years now and I have never experienced a rude staff member until today. Disappointingly when we checked out today Saturday 16th December she was so rude in the way she spoke and her manner. I will be calling on Monday to speak to the manager.
I give five stars to Mother Nature, we all know how wonderful the spot is. How stunning the view is from the camp kitchen, how easy it is to walk to Main Street Noosa etc.
Sadly, under the new management the bathrooms are filthy, at this busy time they should be clean twice in a 24hr period as they have been in years gone by. Cubicles with no toilet paper and no soap in the dispensers. The laundry had 2 if not 3 of the washing machines out of order over our 2 week stay.
The camp kitchen was thick of a slimy oily film on the bench tops and the smell from the fridge in the heat was disgusting.
Sadly people and families will return to this stunning spot because they have been coming for generations but the staff and management need to lift their game.
Zeer onvriendelijk personeel bij de receptie!!

The location of the park is wonderful. It’s right by the water and is a great location for water sports. It’s also an easy walk into Noosa. This was the best part about the park.

Unfortunately, the camp kitchen space was pretty yucky and the fridge was gross. Despite letting the staff know promptly, still nothing was done over the time we stayed to clean the fridge. We found our bag of food in a dirty puddle of water at the bottom of the fridge. It sounded like a build up of water in the fridge was a known issue so maybe some notices on the fridges would have been helpful letting campers know and also to make sure people shut the doors properly. We didn't find much in the way of notices and resources to help campers keep the communal areas tidy. Unfortunately, the camp kitchen tables, cooking top/BBQ and worktops always had food scraps and sticky stuff on them.

If you are self-contained and don’t need to access the camp kitchen at all, then this is would probably be a good place to stay.

Disgusting customer service received this evening 30/11/2023 from a small fair haired female employee.Before approaching reception I noticed multiple spaces free in the camp. I had been ringing since 5pm trying to make a booking for the night to which all my calls went unanswered. My first encounter with the woman was rude, with her telling me abruptly that they were closed (at 5:55pm) and full for the night. Which was just a blatant lie. She tried to explain the empty spaces to which she made zero sense, and then told me “you’ll have to try somewhere else tonight” .
I was unimpressed by this and then asked for her name, which she refused to give me. Not a good experience with the camp at all, and will be looking forward to hearing from management as to why my calls were not answered and why I received such hostility from their member of staff.
Très belle endroit
schöner moderner Campingplatz. sanitäre Anlagen sind top, direkt an Wasser
Beautiful spot...clean amenities...friendly receptionist

Wir haben mehrmals versucht den Campingplatz telefonisch zu erreichen. Leider ist nie jemand ans Telefon gegangen.
Auch auf unsere mehrmalige Bitte uns zurückzurufen, hat sich niemand gemeldet.
Leider sehr sehr enttäuschend und ein schlechter Kundenservice.

Absolutely amazing location. We stayed on a riverside tent location. Overall good facilities and location. However, it was a very disappointing attitude from one staff member from Spain. I wanted to ensure that I was doing everything right by confirming prior to arrival of requirement for my camp site. Another staff member even came out to confirm my set-up and location. The following day, this staff came out and rudely told us that our tent was too far over and my set-up was not approved for the site. I tried to explain the information I was given and staff approval on arrival, but she would not have it. She would not let me finish explaining and rudely talked over me. Very disappointing and disgusting behavior.  Staff requires training in customer service.
Away from the hubbub of Hastings St. but close enough for a 30 minute walk. Farmers market too. Right on the river with great sunsets and fishing. Clean and ample amenities. Book ahead!!
Great park in a fantastic location, easy walk to everything, amenities quite new and clean, definitely visit again,
Le camping est au calme et vue sur les everglades ❤️
Answer your phone how hard is it
Best Beach Access ⛱️ 💙
Nice hot good shower with big space and lots of hooks! May visit again. 👍
Fantastic caravan park! The sites are very generous, the location perfect and the amenities excellent. We had a lovely few days soaking up all Noosa has to offer.
Good facilities and nicely maintained. WiFi didn't work though.
Great place. Right on the river and you can see the beach from the park. Beautiful sand, water clear.
Such a beautiful littel spot, very well laid out, great facilities. Close to the Appolonian for a nice pub meal as well
Very popular - beautiful views - love to return

What a magical spot! We camped in the tent only area and think it was a the best spot! Really great amenities and waterfront views. Kids had a blast on the fishing ski and stand up paddle board. We’ll definitely be back again
Love this place, my favourite Campground. Dog friendly, nice lake, we caught some nice fish too.
Magnifique
Très joli camping au bord de l’eau. Douches et toilettes propres. De belles installations de cuisine. Très proche pour la traversée vers Fraser Island.
I love this place! The placement is beautiful at the river side!

Loved our stay and appreciated them taking up my suggestion of better hand dryers in the amenities blocks 👏 My next suggestion……compost bins for recycling food scraps and a herb or vegie garden near the camp kitchen for sharing. (Look at Kingscliff caravan park for set up ideas!). Thanks again for a lovely stay and see you next year!
Backs onto the River with a sandy beach.
Great spot lake is beautiful spent two nights in van will definitely be back
Un lugar hermoso junto al río , en relación precio calidad me pareció excelente! Estaba todo muy limpio ! El personal excelente!

Nice and quiet, too many trees for solar charging.people need to adhere to rules about dogs and keep them on their leads and pick up after them. Too many dog poos on beach area it is not a leash free area.Very relaxing with good beach access.

Wir fuhren spontan vorbei um zu Fragen ob es noch einen Platz hätte. Die Dame an der Rezeption war sehr unfreundlich und brachte kaum ein lächeln über die Lippen. Die Art und Weise mit sie mit Gästen spricht ist völlig unangebracht.
